feedback.correctItalian: Esatto! Questa è la richiesta standard al controllo.
feedback.incorrectItalian: No, chiedono il passaporto: 'Passaporto, per favore.'
feedback.correctEnglish: Exact! This is the standard request at the check.
feedback.incorrectEnglish: No, they ask for the passport: 'Passport, please.'

feedback.correctItalian: Corretto! Si dice quando si consegna il documento.
feedback.incorrectItalian: Sbagliato. 'Ecco a lei' è la formula per dare qualcosa.
feedback.correctEnglish: Correct! You say this when handing over the document.
feedback.incorrectEnglish: Wrong. 'Ecco a lei' is the formula for giving something.

choicesItalian: Qual è lo scopo del viaggio?
choicesEnglish: What is the purpose of the trip?
correctAnswerItalian: Qual è lo scopo del viaggio?
correctAnswerEnglish: What is the purpose of the trip?

feedback.correctItalian: Ottimo! Una domanda ufficiale molto comune.
feedback.incorrectItalian: No, chiedono il motivo: 'Qual è lo scopo del viaggio?'
feedback.correctEnglish: Great! A very common official question.
feedback.incorrectEnglish: No, they ask for the reason: 'What is the purpose of the trip?'

feedback.correctItalian: Perfetto! La risposta tipica dei turisti.
feedback.correctEnglish: Perfect! The typical response for tourists.
feedback.incorrectEnglish: No, it means 'I'm here on holiday'.

feedback.correctItalian: Esatto! Indica la durata della permanenza.
feedback.incorrectItalian: No, la risposta corretta è 'Per due settimane'.
feedback.correctEnglish: Exact! It indicates the length of the stay.
feedback.incorrectEnglish: No, the correct answer is 'For two weeks'.

feedback.correctItalian: Giusto! Chiedono l'indirizzo del tuo alloggio.
feedback.correctEnglish: Right! They are asking for the address of your stay.
feedback.incorrectEnglish: No, 'alloggerà' refers to accommodation.

feedback.correctItalian: Corretto! Una risposta chiara e precisa.
feedback.correctEnglish: Correct! A clear and precise response.
feedback.incorrectEnglish: Wrong. It means 'In a hotel downtown'.

feedback.correctEnglish: Great! The final wish from the officer.
feedback.incorrectEnglish: No, they say 'buona permanenza' at the end.

choicesEnglish: Do you have anything to declare?
Do you have a lot of money?
correctAnswerEnglish: Do you have anything to declare?

feedback.correctItalian: Perfetto! La domanda classica della dogana.
feedback.correctEnglish: Perfect! The classic customs question.
feedback.incorrectEnglish: No, the correct verb is 'dichiarare'.

feedback.correctItalian: Esatto! La risposta più comune se non hai merci proibite.
feedback.correctEnglish: Exact! The most common response if you have no prohibited goods.
feedback.incorrectEnglish: No, 'nulla' means nothing.

feedback.correctItalian: Giusto! Significa che hai solo oggetti tuoi.
feedback.incorrectItalian: No, si usa la frase 'effetti personali'.
feedback.correctEnglish: Right! It means you only have your own items.
feedback.incorrectEnglish: No, the phrase 'effetti personali' is used.

feedback.incorrectItalian: No, chiedono di aprirla: 'Apra questa valigia'.
feedback.correctEnglish: Correct! A request for inspection.
feedback.incorrectEnglish: No, they ask to open it: 'Open this suitcase'.

feedback.correctEnglish: Great! It shows cooperation.
feedback.incorrectEnglish: No, 'Certamente' is the polite response.

feedback.incorrectItalian: Sbagliato. Si ringrazia per la collaborazione.
feedback.correctEnglish: Perfect! A formal thank you.
feedback.incorrectEnglish: Wrong. We thank you for the collaboration.

choicesEnglish: Do you have an entry visa?
Do you have a valid passport?
Do you have an identity card?
Do you have a special permit?
correctAnswerEnglish: Do you have an entry visa?

feedback.correctItalian: Esatto! Chiede il documento per entrare.
feedback.incorrectItalian: No, 'visto d'ingresso' è la parola chiave.
feedback.correctEnglish: Exact! Asks for the document to enter.
feedback.incorrectEnglish: No, 'visto d'ingresso' is the keyword.

feedback.correctEnglish: Right! Declares your nationality.
feedback.incorrectEnglish: No, it means 'I'm an American citizen'.

choicesEnglish: I'm not looking for a visa.

feedback.incorrectItalian: No, 'non mi serve' significa che non è necessario.
feedback.correctEnglish: Correct! Explains that you are exempt.
feedback.incorrectEnglish: No, 'non mi serve' means it is not necessary.

choicesItalian: Mi mostri il biglietto di ritorno.
correctAnswerItalian: Mi mostri il biglietto di ritorno.

feedback.correctItalian: Ottimo! Una richiesta per verificare la partenza.
feedback.incorrectItalian: No, l'ufficiale vuole vedere il ritorno.
feedback.correctEnglish: Great! A request to verify the departure.
feedback.incorrectEnglish: No, the officer wants to see the return.

feedback.correctItalian: Perfetto! Significa che i documenti vanno bene.
feedback.correctEnglish: Perfect! It means the documents are fine.
feedback.incorrectEnglish: Wrong. It means 'Everything in order'.

feedback.correctItalian: Esatto! Il saluto finale di benvenuto.
feedback.incorrectItalian: No, l'ufficiale ti dà il benvenuto nel paese.
feedback.correctEnglish: Exact! The final welcome greeting.
feedback.incorrectEnglish: No, the officer welcomes you to the country.

feedback.correctEnglish: Right! Indicates the activity in progress.
feedback.incorrectEnglish: No, it's called 'Document check'.

feedback.correctEnglish: Correct! Used to ask for time.
feedback.incorrectEnglish: No, it means 'One moment'.

feedback.incorrectItalian: No, si usa per pensare mentre si parla.
feedback.correctEnglish: Great! A typical spoken hesitation.
feedback.incorrectEnglish: No, it's used to think while speaking.

feedback.correctItalian: Perfetto! Un modo educato di rivolgersi a lui.
feedback.correctEnglish: Perfect! A polite way to address him.
feedback.incorrectEnglish: No, you must be formal: 'officer'.

feedback.correctItalian: Esatto! Una domanda frequente alla frontiera.
feedback.incorrectItalian: No, chiede se è la prima visita.
feedback.correctEnglish: Exact! A frequent question at the border.
feedback.incorrectEnglish: No, it asks if it's the first visit.

feedback.correctEnglish: Right! Short and clear response.
feedback.incorrectEnglish: Wrong. It means 'Yes, the first'.

choicesEnglish: Take the passport out of the case.
correctAnswerEnglish: Take the passport out of the case.

feedback.correctEnglish: Correct! To facilitate scanning.
feedback.incorrectEnglish: No, the officer asks to take it out.

feedback.correctItalian: Ottimo! Istruzione per il riconoscimento facciale.
feedback.correctEnglish: Great! Instruction for facial recognition.
feedback.incorrectEnglish: No, you must look at the lens.

feedback.correctItalian: Perfetto! Per prendere le impronte digitali.
feedback.incorrectItalian: No, chiede di posizionare le dita.
feedback.correctEnglish: Perfect! To take fingerprints.
feedback.incorrectEnglish: No, it asks to position your fingers.

feedback.correctItalian: Esatto! Chiede la durata del soggiorno.
feedback.incorrectItalian: No, chiede la durata: 'Quanto tempo resta?'
feedback.correctEnglish: Exact! Asks for the duration of the stay.
feedback.incorrectEnglish: No, it asks for the duration: 'How long are you staying?'

feedback.correctEnglish: Right! An estimate of the time.
feedback.incorrectEnglish: Wrong. It means 'About ten days'.

choicesEnglish: Do you have cash with you?
correctAnswerEnglish: Do you have cash with you?

feedback.correctItalian: Corretto! Chiedono per i limiti di valuta.
feedback.correctEnglish: Correct! They ask for currency limits.
feedback.incorrectEnglish: No, 'contanti' means cash.

feedback.correctEnglish: Great! A prudent and honest response.
feedback.incorrectEnglish: No, it means 'Only a few euros'.

feedback.incorrectItalian: No, vuoi sapere dove si trova la dogana.
feedback.correctEnglish: Perfect! You're asking for directions.
feedback.incorrectEnglish: No, you want to know where customs is located.

choicesEnglish: At the end on the right.
correctAnswerEnglish: At the end on the right.

feedback.correctItalian: Esatto! Indicazione di posizione molto comune.
feedback.incorrectItalian: Sbagliato. Significa 'At the end on the right'.
feedback.correctEnglish: Exact! A very common position indication.
feedback.incorrectEnglish: Wrong. It means 'At the end on the right'.

feedback.incorrectItalian: No, ti dice di proseguire: 'Vada pure avanti'.
feedback.correctEnglish: Right! Permission to proceed.
feedback.incorrectEnglish: No, it tells you to continue: 'Go ahead'.

feedback.incorrectItalian: No, ti chiede di aspettare il tuo turno.
feedback.correctEnglish: Correct! Common airport order.
feedback.incorrectEnglish: No, it asks you to wait your turn.

feedback.correctEnglish: Great! Question about the profession.
feedback.incorrectEnglish: No, it asks about your occupation.

feedback.correctEnglish: Perfect! Declares your profession.
feedback.incorrectEnglish: Wrong. It means 'I'm a teacher'.

feedback.incorrectItalian: No, è la frase di chiusura positiva.
feedback.correctEnglish: Exact! Positive conclusion of the check.
feedback.incorrectEnglish: No, it's the positive closing phrase.

feedback.correctItalian: Giusto! L'augurio standard per chi parte.
feedback.correctEnglish: Right! The standard wish for someone leaving.
feedback.incorrectEnglish: No, it means 'Have a good trip'.

choicesEnglish: Can you lend me a pen?
correctAnswerEnglish: Can you lend me a pen?

feedback.incorrectItalian: No, chiedi una penna in prestito.
feedback.correctEnglish: Correct! To fill out the form.
feedback.incorrectEnglish: No, you're asking to borrow a pen.

feedback.correctEnglish: Great! Affirmative and kind response.
feedback.incorrectEnglish: Wrong. It means 'Sure, here it is'.

feedback.correctItalian: Perfetto! Chiedi della durata della procedura.
feedback.incorrectItalian: No, vuoi sapere il tempo necessario.
feedback.correctEnglish: Perfect! You're asking about the procedure duration.
feedback.incorrectEnglish: No, you want to know the time needed.

feedback.correctEnglish: Exact! Reassuring response about the time.
feedback.incorrectEnglish: Wrong. It means 'A few more minutes'.

choicesItalian: Qual è la sua data di nascita?
correctAnswerItalian: Qual è la sua data di nascita?

feedback.incorrectItalian: No, l'ufficiale chiede la data di nascita.
feedback.correctEnglish: Right! A fundamental personal data question.
feedback.incorrectEnglish: No, the officer asks for the birth date.

feedback.correctItalian: Corretto! Risposta con una data specifica.
feedback.correctEnglish: Correct! Response with a specific date.
feedback.incorrectEnglish: Wrong. It means 'May twelfth'.

feedback.correctItalian: Ottimo! Chiedi dove prendere le valigie.
feedback.correctEnglish: Great! You're asking where to get your bags.
feedback.incorrectEnglish: No, you're looking for 'baggage claim'.

feedback.correctEnglish: Perfect! Indication of the building floor.
feedback.incorrectEnglish: Wrong. It means 'On the ground floor'.

feedback.correctItalian: Esatto! Utile se hai difficoltà con l'italiano.
feedback.incorrectItalian: No, significa 'Sorry, I don't understand well'.
feedback.correctEnglish: Exact! Useful if you're having trouble with Italian.
feedback.incorrectEnglish: No, it means 'Sorry, I don't understand well'.

feedback.correctItalian: Giusto! Richiesta di un documento specifico.
feedback.incorrectItalian: No, l'ufficiale vuole vedere il visto.
feedback.correctEnglish: Right! Request for a specific document.
feedback.incorrectEnglish: No, the officer wants to see the visa.

feedback.correctItalian: Corretto! Un grosso problema per il viaggiatore.
feedback.correctEnglish: Correct! A big problem for the traveler.
feedback.incorrectEnglish: Wrong. It means 'It expired yesterday'.

choicesEnglish: Do I have to pay anything?
correctAnswerEnglish: Do I have to pay anything?

feedback.correctItalian: Ottimo! Chiedi se ci sono tasse d'ingresso.
feedback.incorrectItalian: No, chiedi se c'è un pagamento da fare.
feedback.correctEnglish: Great! You're asking if there are entry fees.
feedback.incorrectEnglish: No, you're asking if there's a payment to be made.

feedback.correctEnglish: Perfect! No additional cost.
feedback.incorrectEnglish: Wrong. It means 'No, it's all free'.

choicesEnglish: Welcome, I hope you like it.
Have a good flight, feel free to leave.
correctAnswerEnglish: Welcome, I hope you like it.

feedback.incorrectItalian: No, è un augurio di benvenuto gentile.
feedback.correctEnglish: Exact! A very cordial welcome.
feedback.incorrectEnglish: No, it's a kind welcome wish.

feedback.incorrectItalian: No, significa 'I'm looking for my hotel'.
feedback.correctEnglish: Right! A common concern upon arrival.
feedback.incorrectEnglish: No, it means 'I'm looking for my hotel'.

feedback.correctItalian: Corretto! Specifica il nome del posto.
feedback.correctEnglish: Correct! Specifies the name of the place.
feedback.incorrectEnglish: Wrong. It means 'It's called Hotel Nazionale'.

feedback.correctEnglish: Great! Indicates the accommodation area.
feedback.incorrectEnglish: No, it means 'In the city center'.

feedback.incorrectItalian: No, chiede cosa fai per vivere.
feedback.correctEnglish: Perfect! Formal question about work.
feedback.incorrectEnglish: No, it asks what you do for a living.

feedback.correctItalian: Esatto! Una risposta comune per i giovani.
feedback.correctEnglish: Exact! A common response for young people.
feedback.incorrectEnglish: Wrong. It means 'I am a student'.

feedback.correctItalian: Giusto! Specifica la natura della visita.
feedback.correctEnglish: Right! Specifies the nature of the visit.
feedback.incorrectEnglish: No, it means 'For short stays'.

feedback.incorrectItalian: No, significa che i documenti sono ok.
feedback.correctEnglish: Correct! Standard confirmation phrase.
feedback.incorrectEnglish: No, it means the documents are ok.

feedback.incorrectItalian: No, ti dà il benvenuto nel paese.
feedback.correctEnglish: Great! The final greeting from the officer.
feedback.incorrectEnglish: No, he welcomes you to the country.

choicesEnglish: Do you have anything to declare?
Do you have a lot of money?
correctAnswerEnglish: Do you have anything to declare?

feedback.incorrectItalian: No, chiede se devi pagare tasse sulle merci.
feedback.correctEnglish: Perfect! Mandatory question at customs.
feedback.incorrectEnglish: No, it asks if you have to pay taxes on goods.

feedback.correctEnglish: Exact! Polite negative response.
feedback.incorrectEnglish: Wrong. It means 'No, nothing. Thanks'.

feedback.correctItalian: Giusto! Richiesta di ispezione di una borsa.
feedback.incorrectItalian: No, l'ufficiale chiede di aprire la valigia.
feedback.correctEnglish: Right! Request for inspection of a bag.
feedback.incorrectEnglish: No, the officer asks to open the suitcase.

feedback.correctEnglish: Correct! Immediate collaboration.
feedback.incorrectEnglish: Wrong. It means 'Certainly, here it is'.

feedback.correctEnglish: Great! Formal concluding thank you.
feedback.incorrectEnglish: No, it thanks for the help received.

feedback.correctItalian: Perfetto! Chiede il documento di ingresso.
feedback.correctEnglish: Perfect! Asks for the entry document.
feedback.incorrectEnglish: No, it refers to the entry 'visa'.
